title: Sampling, Inference, Regression, and Causal Limits · Independent Model Trial
summary: Evaluate a program change from sampled observational and experimental evidence. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of an inference and limitations brief with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish observational studies, experiments, sampling frames, randomization, and blocking.
Explain sampling variability through simulated sampling distributions.
Construct and interpret confidence intervals without probability misstatements.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper
